prompt: A request sends a 6,000-token prompt and receives a 200-token answer. Input is priced at $5 per million tokens and output at $15 per million tokens. Roughly what does this request cost?
Which of the following typically INCREASE the latency of a single LLM feature? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Adding a retrieval/tool step before the model answers
Streaming the response to the user

prompt: Which statements about LLM cost and latency optimization are correct? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Prompt caching avoids re-paying to process a stable prompt prefix
Streaming reduces the total number of tokens generated
Routing easy tasks to a smaller model can cut both cost and latency
Output tokens are usually priced higher than input tokens

prompt: A PM is told a chat feature feels slow even though total response time is acceptable for the use case, and the budget is tight. Which change best improves PERCEIVED speed WITHOUT increasing cost?
choices: Switch to a larger, more capable model
Stream the response so tokens appear as they are generated
Remove prompt caching to simplify the pipeline
